title: freestiler
text: Rust-powered, MLT-first vector tile engine R. Creates PMTiles archives sf objects zero external dependencies (tippecanoe, Java, Go).

title: freestiler
text: freestiler takes sf data frame produces .pmtiles file containing vector tiles. supports two tile formats: MLT (MapLibre Tiles) — default. next-gen columnar binary format announced Jan 2026. one first MLT encoders outside reference Java/C++ implementations. MVT (Mapbox Vector Tiles) — protobuf-based, supported everywhere. Used compatibility fallback. entire pipeline runs -memory: sf → Rust (via extendr) → tile encoding → PMTiles archive. temp files, shelling .

title: freestiler
code: R: freestile(sf_obj, "output.pmtiles")   → .decompose_geometries() extracts flat coordinate vectors from sf   → .extract_properties() extracts typed attribute columns   → rust_freestile() calls into Rust via extendr  Rust:   lib.rs       — extendr entry point, parses R vectors into Feature structs   tiler.rs     — tile math (lon/lat ↔ tile x/y), assigns features to tiles per zoom   clip.rs      — clips geometries to tile boundaries (Cohen-Sutherland for lines, Sutherland-Hodgman for polygons)   simplify.rs  — tile-pixel grid snapping (prevents slivers, zoom-adaptive simplification)   mvt.rs       — MVT protobuf encoder (prost-based, hand-written structs matching vector_tile.proto)   mlt.rs       — MLT columnar binary encoder (varint, delta encoding, typed property streams)   pmtiles_writer.rs — assembles tiles into PMTiles v3 archive with gzip compression

title: freestiler
text: Build system: Copied spopt-r (/Users/kylewalker/Library/CloudStorage/Dropbox/dev/spopt-r). Uses configure/configure.win → tools/config.R pattern Makevars.placeholder replacement. CRAN-ready vendor tarball support. extendr 0.8: R ↔︎ Rust bridge. #[extendr] function rust_freestile receives flat vectors (coords, offsets, typed property columns) R. Geometry decomposition: R side (freestile.R) decomposes sf geometries flat coordinate arrays offset vectors rings/parts. Important: uses sf::st_geometry_type() get geometry types, class(g)[1] (returns “XY”). MLT tile_type patching: pmtiles2 crate doesn’t MLT (0x06) TileType enum, write MVT patch byte 99 PMTiles header 0x06 writing. Parallel tiling: Uses rayon two-level parallelism — across tiles within tiles (features processed parallel). Critical low-zoom tiles containing many features. Simplification: Uses tile-pixel grid snapping instead Douglas-Peucker. Coordinates rounded tile’s 4096×4096 pixel grid consecutive duplicates removed. prevents slivers adjacent polygons (shared vertices snap pixel) provides natural zoom-adaptive simplification. Polygon clipping: Sutherland-Hodgman O(4n) rectangle clipping, bbox fast-paths fully-contained fully-outside polygons. Replaced original geo::BooleanOps intersection O((n+k)log n). Tiny feature dropping: Sub-pixel features (bbox < 1 pixel current zoom) skipped lower zoom levels, dramatically reducing work large datasets.

title: freestiler
code: # From within the freestiler directory: NOT_CRAN=true devtools::load_all() devtools::test()  # Quick manual test: library(sf) nc <- st_read(system.file("shape/nc.shp", package = "sf")) freestile(nc, "test.pmtiles", layer_name = "counties") freestile(nc, "test_mvt.pmtiles", layer_name = "counties", tile_format = "mvt")

title: freestiler
code: freestile(   input,                    # sf object   output,                   # Output .pmtiles path   layer_name = NULL,        # Layer name (derived from filename if NULL)   tile_format = "mlt",      # "mlt" (default) or "mvt"   min_zoom = 0,   max_zoom = 14,   simplification = TRUE,    # Grid-snap to tile pixels (prevents slivers)   generate_ids = TRUE,      # Sequential feature IDs   overwrite = TRUE,   quiet = FALSE )

title: freestiler
text: MVP complete tests pass (10/10) Supports: POINT, MULTIPOINT, LINESTRING, MULTILINESTRING, POLYGON, MULTIPOLYGON Property types: character, integer, numeric, logical (NA handling) Auto-transforms WGS84 input CRS differs PMTiles headers verified correct (tile_type=1 MVT, tile_type=6 MLT, version=3)

title: freestiler
text: Fix tile boundary seam artifacts (visible zoom 6-8; geometry verified correct adjacent tiles, winding correct, dropped features — root cause still investigation) base_zoom parameter: zoom level features stop dropped, ensuring features present client-side counting/querying (inspired tippecanoe’s --base-zoom / -B) Advanced MLT encodings (FastPFOR, FSST) Direct file input (GeoJSON, GeoPackage) Integration pmtiles package (pm_create(engine = "freestiler"))

First get Python correct stable.

title: Getting Started
text: Install r-universe: r-universe build includes Rust DuckDB backend macOS Linux, powers streaming point pipeline freestile_query(). can also install GitHub devtools::install_github("walkerke/freestiler"). Python, see Python Setup article.
code: install.packages(   "freestiler",   repos = c("https://walkerke.r-universe.dev", "https://cloud.r-project.org") )

title: Getting Started
text: main function freestile(). Let’s tile North Carolina counties dataset ships sf: ’s useful verifying installation, let’s try something interesting.
code: library(sf) library(freestiler)  nc <- st_read(system.file("shape/nc.shp", package = "sf"))  freestile(nc, "nc_counties.pmtiles", layer_name = "counties") Creating MLT tiles (zoom 0-14) for 100 features across 1 layer...   Tiling layer 'counties' (zoom 0-14)... Created nc_counties.pmtiles (65.2 KB)

title: Getting Started
text: Let’s tile 242,000 US block groups tigris package. takes 20 seconds machine produces tileset can zoom national level individual neighborhoods:
code: library(tigris) options(tigris_use_cache = TRUE)  bgs <- block_groups(cb = TRUE)  freestile(   bgs,   "us_bgs.pmtiles",   layer_name = "bgs",   min_zoom = 4,   max_zoom = 12 )

title: Getting Started
text: quickest way view tileset view_tiles(), starts local HTTP server creates interactive map one step: auto-detects layer name geometry type PMTiles metadata, works box polygon, line, point data. control styling layers, use serve_tiles() start server separately build map mapgl. See Mapping mapgl article full walkthrough workflow. built-server handles CORS HTTP range requests automatically. tilesets larger ~1 GB, ’ll get better performance external server:
code: view_tiles("us_bgs.pmtiles") npx http-server /path/to/tiles -p 8082 --cors -c-1

title: Getting Started
text: default tile format MLT, tends produce smaller files polygon-heavy data. need maximum viewer compatibility - particularly Python viewers older MapLibre versions - use MVT:
code: freestile(nc, "nc_mvt.pmtiles", layer_name = "counties", tile_format = "mvt")

title: Getting Started
text: Use min_zoom max_zoom set zoom range tileset:
code: freestile(nc, "nc_z4_10.pmtiles",   layer_name = "counties",   min_zoom = 4,   max_zoom = 10 )

title: Getting Started
text: large datasets, drop_rate provides exponential feature thinning lower zoom levels. Points thinned using spatial ordering maintain even coverage; polygons lines thinned area. base_zoom parameter controls zoom level features kept:
code: freestile(nc, "nc_dropping.pmtiles",   layer_name = "counties",   drop_rate = 2.5,   base_zoom = 10 )

title: Getting Started
text: can tile spatial files disk without loading R first. useful large GeoParquet files formats ’d rather pull memory:
code: # GeoParquet freestile_file("census_blocks.parquet", "blocks.pmtiles")  # GeoPackage, Shapefile, or other formats via DuckDB freestile_file("counties.gpkg", "counties.pmtiles", engine = "duckdb")

title: Getting Started
text: data already lives DuckDB, can run SQL query pipe results directly tiling engine. lets filter, join, transform data SQL tiling: large point datasets, set streaming = "always" use streaming pipeline, avoids loading full query result memory: recent run, streamed 146 million US job points DuckDB 2.3 GB PMTiles archive 12 minutes.
code: freestile_query(   "SELECT * FROM ST_Read('counties.shp') WHERE pop > 50000",   "large_counties.pmtiles" ) freestile_query(   query = "SELECT naics, state, ST_Point(lon, lat) AS geometry FROM jobs_dots",   output = "us_jobs_dots.pmtiles",   db_path = db_path,   layer_name = "jobs",   tile_format = "mvt",   min_zoom = 4,   max_zoom = 14,   base_zoom = 14,   drop_rate = 2.5,   source_crs = "EPSG:4326",   streaming = "always",   overwrite = TRUE )

title: Getting Started
text: Pass named list create multi-layer tilesets. Use freestile_layer() want per-layer zoom control:
code: pts <- st_centroid(nc)  freestile(   list(     counties = freestile_layer(nc, min_zoom = 0, max_zoom = 10),     centroids = freestile_layer(pts, min_zoom = 6, max_zoom = 14)   ),   "nc_layers.pmtiles" )

title: Getting Started
text: point layers, cluster_distance merges nearby points clusters point_count attribute:
code: freestile(pts, "nc_clustered.pmtiles",   layer_name = "centroids",   cluster_distance = 50,   cluster_maxzoom = 8 )

title: Getting Started
text: coalesce parameter merges features identical attributes within tile. Lines sharing endpoints joined, polygons grouped MultiPolygons:
code: freestile(nc, "nc_coalesced.pmtiles",   layer_name = "counties",   coalesce = TRUE )

title: MapLibre Tiles (MLT)
text: Vector tiles divide geographic data grid square tiles multiple zoom levels. Unlike raster tiles (pre-rendered images), vector tiles store actual geometry attribute data, client can style, interact , dynamically render data. standard container vector tilesets PMTiles - single-file archive can serve static file host.

title: MapLibre Tiles (MLT)
text: Mapbox Vector Tiles (MVT) dominant vector tile encoding since Mapbox introduced 2014. uses Protocol Buffers serialize geometry attributes, ’s supported every major mapping library: MapLibre GL, Mapbox GL, Leaflet, deck.gl, . need maximum compatibility, MVT safe choice.

title: MapLibre Tiles (MLT)
text: MVT stores data row--row (one feature time), MLT organizes data column--column - x-coordinates together, y-coordinates together, values given attribute together. columnar layout opens compression techniques aren’t available MVT’s row-oriented format: delta encoding coordinates, run-length encoding repeated values, dictionary encoding string columns low cardinality. practical result smaller tiles, especially polygon-heavy datasets: Point-datasets see minimal savings coordinate data dominates compresses similarly formats.

title: MapLibre Tiles (MLT)
text: Use MLT (default) ’re working R mapgl viewing browser MapLibre GL JS 5.17+. MLT produces smaller files polygon line data, mapgl supports natively. Use MVT need widest viewer compatibility - particularly Python-facing examples, older MapLibre versions, sharing tilesets users tools don’t control. can switch formats single argument:
code: freestile(nc, "nc_mvt.pmtiles", layer_name = "counties", tile_format = "mvt")

title: MapLibre Tiles (MLT)
text: MLT still new (January 2026), library support growing: Encoding: freestiler (Rust, R + Python), mlt-core reference encoder (Java/C++) Decoding: MapLibre GL JS (experimental), mlt-core decoder Viewing: MapLibre GL JS MLT plugin, mapgl R MLT support expands across mapping libraries, default become directly viewable places without needing switch MVT.

title: Mapping with mapgl
text: First, pull block group geometries income data tidycensus: actual tiling takes minutes produces ~250 MB tileset. output include:
code: library(tidycensus) library(freestiler) options(tigris_use_cache = TRUE)  # Pulls data state-by-state, so will take a few minutes if  # geometries are not pre-cached bgs <- get_acs(   geography = "block group",   variables = "B19013_001",  # median household income   state = c(state.abb, "DC"),   year = 2024,   geometry = TRUE )  freestile(   bgs,   "us_income.pmtiles",   layer_name = "income",   min_zoom = 4,   max_zoom = 12 ) Created us_income.pmtiles (252.6 MB) View with: view_tiles("us_income.pmtiles")

title: Mapping with mapgl
text: fast look tileset, use view_tiles():  auto-detects layer name, geometry type, bounds PMTiles metadata, starts local server, opens interactive map. works polygon, line, point data without extra configuration. FYI, specific example - given USA’s bounding box, ’ll need pan continental US view .
code: view_tiles("us_income.pmtiles")

title: Mapping with mapgl
text: control styling, use serve_tiles() start local server, build map mapgl. PMTiles require server supports CORS HTTP range requests — serve_tiles() handles . Now add tileset source style :  Key components: add_pmtiles_source() registers PMTiles file vector tile source. promote_id parameter tells MapLibre property use feature ID (needed hover/click interactivity). source_layer must match layer_name used freestile(). interpolate() creates data-driven color ramp. MapLibre evaluates per-feature GPU, ’s fast even 242K polygons. hover_options highlights features mouseover. requires promote_id set source. tooltip uses concat() number_format() build formatted hover text feature properties.
code: library(mapgl)  serve_tiles("us_income.pmtiles") maplibre(   style = openfreemap_style("positron"),   bounds = c(-125, 24, -66, 50),  ) |>   add_pmtiles_source(     id = "income-src",     url = "http://localhost:8080/us_income.pmtiles",     promote_id = "GEOID"   ) |>   add_fill_layer(     id = "income-fill",     source = "income-src",     source_layer = "income",     fill_color = interpolate(       column = "estimate",       values = c(20000, 50000, 100000, 200000),       stops = c("#d73027", "#fee08b", "#91cf60", "#1a9850"),       na_color = "#cccccc"     ),     fill_opacity = 0.7,     hover_options = list(       fill_opacity = 1     ),     tooltip = concat(       "Median income: $",       number_format(get_column("estimate"), locale = "en")     )   )

title: Mapping with mapgl
text: line data, use add_line_layer(): point data, use add_circle_layer():
code: maplibre() |>   add_pmtiles_source(     id = "roads-src",     url = "http://localhost:8080/roads.pmtiles"   ) |>   add_line_layer(     id = "roads",     source = "roads-src",     source_layer = "roads",     line_color = "#4a90d9",     line_width = 1.5   ) maplibre() |>   add_pmtiles_source(     id = "pts-src",     url = "http://localhost:8080/airports.pmtiles"   ) |>   add_circle_layer(     id = "airports",     source = "pts-src",     source_layer = "airports",     circle_color = "orange",     circle_radius = 5,     circle_stroke_color = "white",     circle_stroke_width = 1   )

title: Mapping with mapgl
text: tileset multiple layers (created named list freestile()), add layer separately — share source:
code: maplibre() |>   add_pmtiles_source(     id = "nc-src",     url = "http://localhost:8080/nc_layers.pmtiles"   ) |>   add_fill_layer(     id = "counties",     source = "nc-src",     source_layer = "counties",     fill_color = "navy",     fill_opacity = 0.3   ) |>   add_circle_layer(     id = "centroids",     source = "nc-src",     source_layer = "centroids",     circle_color = "red",     circle_radius = 4   )

title: Mapping with mapgl
text: freestiler defaults MapLibre Tiles (MLT), produces smaller files. view_tiles() mapgl handle MLT natively recent versions MapLibre GL JS. ’re sharing tiles tools viewers, use tile_format = "mvt" widest compatibility:
code: freestile(bgs, "us_income_mvt.pmtiles",   layer_name = "income",   tile_format = "mvt" )

title: Mapping with mapgl
text: built-serve_tiles() server works well files 1 GB. larger tilesets, use external server better concurrency. terminal, assuming Node’s http-server installed: point add_pmtiles_source() URL http://localhost:8082/... instead. Stop external server Ctrl+C terminal ’re done. ’ll see better performance method built-server datasets 1GB well, like block groups dataset example.
code: npx http-server /path/to/tiles -p 8082 --cors -c-1

title: Mapping with mapgl
text: ’re done viewing built-server, stop :
code: stop_server()

title: Python Setup
text: Install PyPI: Published wheels include full feature set: GeoPandas input, direct file tiling, DuckDB-backed file input, SQL query support. Supported wheel targets Python 3.9 3.14.
code: pip install freestiler

title: Python Setup
text: need Rust toolchain wheel isn’t available platform want editable local build:
code: git clone https://github.com/walkerke/freestiler.git cd freestiler/python python3 -m venv .venv source .venv/bin/activate pip install maturin python3 -m maturin develop --release

title: Python Setup
text: Python API mirrors R closely. ’s equivalent North Carolina example Getting Started article: Multi-layer tilesets use dictionary instead R’s named list: File input DuckDB queries work way:
code: import geopandas as gpd from freestiler import freestile  url = "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/r-spatial/sf/main/inst/gpkg/nc.gpkg" gdf = gpd.read_file(url)  freestile(gdf, "nc_counties.pmtiles", layer_name="counties") from freestiler import freestile, freestile_layer  centroids = gdf.copy() centroids.geometry = gdf.geometry.centroid  freestile(     {         "counties": freestile_layer(gdf, min_zoom=0, max_zoom=10),         "centroids": freestile_layer(centroids, min_zoom=6, max_zoom=14),     },     "nc_layers.pmtiles" ) from freestiler import freestile_file, freestile_query  freestile_file("census_blocks.parquet", "blocks.pmtiles")  freestile_query(     query="SELECT * FROM read_parquet('blocks.parquet') WHERE state = 'NC'",     output="nc_blocks.pmtiles",     layer_name="blocks" )

title: Python Setup
text: freestile(gdf, ...) convenient Python entry point, preprocessing GeoPandas Rust starts tiling. large datasets, freestile_file() freestile_query() usually faster path. practice, expensive part GeoDataFrame path often reprojection WGS84 plus geometry serialization Rust tiler takes . data already disk DuckDB, prefer paths serious workloads.

title: Python Setup
text: ’d recommend creating tiles tile_format="mvt" Python-facing work now. Python viewer stacks still catching MLT support, MVT works everywhere. One important caveat: Python’s built-http.server support byte-range requests, won’t work PMTiles server. ’ll want use real static file server instead: can view PMTiles file (MLT MVT) browser MapLibre GL JS 5.17+ PMTiles protocol. ’re also R user, mapgl package reliable local viewing path right now. recommendation Python users: try Positron IDE, supports Python R simultaneously. ’ll able tiling Python easily move R mapping mapgl package. Read mapping vignette learn .
code: npx http-server /path/to/tiles -p 8082 --cors -c-1

title: freestiler
text: freestiler creates PMTiles vector tilesets R Python. Give sf object, file disk, DuckDB SQL query, writes single .pmtiles file can serve anywhere. tiling engine written Rust runs -process, ’s nothing else install.

title: freestiler
text: Install r-universe: install GitHub:
code: install.packages(   "freestiler",   repos = c("https://walkerke.r-universe.dev", "https://cloud.r-project.org") ) # install.packages("devtools") devtools::install_github("walkerke/freestiler")

title: freestiler
text: Published PyPI wheels currently target Python 3.9 3.14. See Python Setup article details.
code: pip install freestiler

title: freestiler
text: main function freestile(). Let’s tile North Carolina counties dataset ships sf: ’s useful checking installation, API handles much bigger data. tile 242,000 US block groups tigris:
code: library(sf) library(freestiler)  nc <- st_read(system.file("shape/nc.shp", package = "sf"))  freestile(nc, "nc_counties.pmtiles", layer_name = "counties") library(tigris) options(tigris_use_cache = TRUE)  bgs <- block_groups(cb = TRUE)  freestile(   bgs,   "us_bgs.pmtiles",   layer_name = "bgs",   min_zoom = 4,   max_zoom = 12 )

title: freestiler
text: quickest way view tileset view_tiles(), starts local server opens interactive map: control, use serve_tiles() start local server build map mapgl: built-server handles CORS range requests automatically. tilesets larger ~1 GB, use external server like npx http-server /path --cors -c-1 better performance. See Mapping mapgl article full walkthrough.
code: view_tiles("us_bgs.pmtiles") library(mapgl)  serve_tiles("us_bgs.pmtiles")  maplibre(hash = TRUE) |>   add_pmtiles_source(     id = "bgs-src",     url = "http://localhost:8080/us_bgs.pmtiles",     promote_id = "GEOID"   ) |>   add_fill_layer(     id = "bgs-fill",     source = "bgs-src",     source_layer = "bgs",     fill_color = "navy",     fill_opacity = 0.5,     hover_options = list(       fill_color = "#ffffcc",       fill_opacity = 0.9     )   )

title: freestiler
text: data lives DuckDB, freestile_query() lets filter, join, transform SQL tiling: large point datasets, streaming pipeline avoids loading full result memory. recent run, freestile_query() streamed 146 million US job points DuckDB 2.3 GB PMTiles archive 12 minutes:
code: freestile_query(   query = "SELECT * FROM read_parquet('blocks.parquet') WHERE state = 'NC'",   output = "nc_blocks.pmtiles",   layer_name = "blocks" ) freestile_query(   query = "SELECT naics, state, ST_Point(lon, lat) AS geometry FROM jobs_dots",   output = "us_jobs_dots.pmtiles",   db_path = db_path,   layer_name = "jobs",   tile_format = "mvt",   min_zoom = 4,   max_zoom = 14,   base_zoom = 14,   drop_rate = 2.5,   source_crs = "EPSG:4326",   streaming = "always",   overwrite = TRUE )

title: freestiler
text: can tile spatial files without loading R first:
code: # GeoParquet freestile_file("census_blocks.parquet", "blocks.pmtiles")  # GeoPackage, Shapefile, or other formats via DuckDB freestile_file("counties.gpkg", "counties.pmtiles", engine = "duckdb")

title: freestiler
code: pts <- st_centroid(nc)  freestile(   list(     counties = freestile_layer(nc, min_zoom = 0, max_zoom = 10),     centroids = freestile_layer(pts, min_zoom = 6, max_zoom = 14)   ),   "nc_layers.pmtiles" )

title: freestiler
text: freestiler defaults MapLibre Tiles (MLT), columnar encoding produces smaller files polygon line data. Use tile_format = "mvt" need widest viewer compatibility.

what: Create vector tiles from spatial data — freestile
title: Create vector tiles from spatial data — freestile
text: Creates PMTiles archive containing vector tiles one sf data frames. Supports Mapbox Vector Tile (MVT) MapLibre Tile (MLT) formats, multi-layer output, feature dropping, point clustering, feature coalescing.

title: Create vector tiles from spatial data — freestile
code: freestile(   input,   output,   layer_name = NULL,   tile_format = "mlt",   min_zoom = 0L,   max_zoom = 14L,   base_zoom = NULL,   drop_rate = NULL,   cluster_distance = NULL,   cluster_maxzoom = NULL,   coalesce = FALSE,   simplification = TRUE,   generate_ids = TRUE,   overwrite = TRUE,   quiet = FALSE )

title: Create vector tiles from spatial data — freestile
text: input sf data frame, named list sf/freestile_layer objects multi-layer output. output Character. Path output .pmtiles file. layer_name Character. Name tile layer. NULL, derived output filename. used single-layer input. tile_format Character. Tile encoding format: "mlt" (default) MapLibre Tiles "mvt" Mapbox Vector Tiles. min_zoom Integer. Minimum zoom level (default 0). max_zoom Integer. Maximum zoom level (default 14). base_zoom Integer. Zoom level features present (dropping). NULL (default) uses layer's max_zoom. drop-rate curve also computed relative base_zoom, lowering produces gentler thinning low zooms. Inspired tippecanoe's -B / --base-zoom. drop_rate Numeric. Exponential drop rate feature thinning (e.g. 2.5). zoom level base_zoom, features retained rate 1/drop_rate^(base_zoom - zoom). Points thinned using spatial ordering; polygons/lines thinned area. NULL (default) disables drop-rate thinning. cluster_distance Numeric. Pixel distance point clustering. Points within radius merged cluster features point_count attribute. NULL (default) disables clustering. cluster_maxzoom Integer. Maximum zoom level clustering. zoom, individual points shown. Default max_zoom - 1. coalesce Logical. Whether merge features identical attributes within tile (default FALSE). Lines sharing endpoints merged; polygons grouped MultiPolygons. simplification Logical. Whether snap geometries tile pixel grid zoom level (default TRUE). provides zoom-adaptive simplification prevents slivers adjacent polygons. generate_ids Logical. Whether assign sequential feature IDs (default TRUE). overwrite Logical. Whether overwrite existing output file (default TRUE). quiet Logical. Whether suppress progress messages (default FALSE).

title: Create vector tiles from spatial data — freestile
text: output file path (invisibly).

title: Create vector tiles from spatial data — freestile
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ library(sf) nc <- st_read(system.file("shape/nc.shp", package = "sf"))  # Single layer freestile(nc, "nc.pmtiles", layer_name = "counties")  # Multi-layer pts <- st_centroid(nc) freestile(   list(counties = nc, centroids = pts),   "nc_layers.pmtiles" )  # With dropping and coalescing freestile(nc, "nc_drop.pmtiles", drop_rate = 2.5, coalesce = TRUE)  # With point clustering freestile(pts, "pts.pmtiles", cluster_distance = 50, cluster_maxzoom = 8) } # }

what: Create vector tiles from a spatial file — freestile_file
title: Create vector tiles from a spatial file — freestile_file
text: Reads GeoParquet, GeoPackage, Shapefile, spatial file directly tiling engine. GeoParquet engine requires compilation FREESTILER_GEOPARQUET=true. DuckDB engine uses Rust DuckDB backend included build (enabled default native builds), falls back R duckdb package (reads file via DuckDB's ST_Read(), auto-detects source CRS via ST_Read_Meta(), reprojects WGS84). Control backend selection options(freestiler.duckdb_backend = "auto"|"rust"|"r").

title: Create vector tiles from a spatial file — freestile_file
code: freestile_file(   input,   output,   layer_name = NULL,   tile_format = "mlt",   min_zoom = 0L,   max_zoom = 14L,   base_zoom = NULL,   drop_rate = NULL,   cluster_distance = NULL,   cluster_maxzoom = NULL,   coalesce = FALSE,   simplification = TRUE,   overwrite = TRUE,   quiet = FALSE,   engine = "geoparquet" )

title: Create vector tiles from a spatial file — freestile_file
text: input Character. Path input spatial file. output Character. Path output .pmtiles file. layer_name Character. Name tile layer. NULL, derived output filename. tile_format Character. "mlt" (default) "mvt". min_zoom Integer. Minimum zoom level (default 0). max_zoom Integer. Maximum zoom level (default 14). base_zoom Integer. Zoom level features present. NULL (default) uses max_zoom. drop_rate Numeric. Exponential drop rate. NULL (default) disables. cluster_distance Numeric. Pixel distance clustering. NULL disables. cluster_maxzoom Integer. Max zoom clustering. Default max_zoom - 1. coalesce Logical. Whether merge features identical attributes (default FALSE). simplification Logical. Whether snap geometries tile pixel grid (default TRUE). overwrite Logical. Whether overwrite existing output (default TRUE). quiet Logical. Whether suppress progress (default FALSE). engine Character. Backend engine: "geoparquet" (default, GeoParquet files) "duckdb" (file format DuckDB supports).

title: Create vector tiles from a spatial file — freestile_file
text: output file path (invisibly).

title: Create vector tiles from a spatial file — freestile_file
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ freestile_file("data.parquet", "output.pmtiles") freestile_file("data.gpkg", "output.pmtiles", engine = "duckdb") } # }

what: Create a layer specification with per-layer zoom range — freestile_layer
title: Create a layer specification with per-layer zoom range — freestile_layer
text: Wraps sf object optional per-layer zoom range overrides use multi-layer tile generation.

title: Create a layer specification with per-layer zoom range — freestile_layer
code: freestile_layer(input, min_zoom = NULL, max_zoom = NULL)

title: Create a layer specification with per-layer zoom range — freestile_layer
text: input sf data frame. min_zoom Integer. Minimum zoom level layer. NULL, uses global min_zoom freestile(). max_zoom Integer. Maximum zoom level layer. NULL, uses global max_zoom freestile().

title: Create a layer specification with per-layer zoom range — freestile_layer
text: freestile_layer object (list class attribute).

title: Create a layer specification with per-layer zoom range — freestile_layer
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ library(sf) nc <- st_read(system.file("shape/nc.shp", package = "sf")) roads <- st_read("roads.shp")  freestile(   list(     counties = freestile_layer(nc, min_zoom = 0, max_zoom = 10),     roads = freestile_layer(roads, min_zoom = 8, max_zoom = 14)   ),   "layers.pmtiles" ) } # }

what: Create vector tiles from a DuckDB SQL query — freestile_query
title: Create vector tiles from a DuckDB SQL query — freestile_query
text: Executes SQL query via DuckDB's spatial extension pipes results tiling engine. Uses Rust DuckDB backend included build (enabled default native builds), falls back R duckdb package. Control backend selection options(freestiler.duckdb_backend = "auto"|"rust"|"r").

title: Create vector tiles from a DuckDB SQL query — freestile_query
code: freestile_query(   query,   output,   db_path = NULL,   layer_name = NULL,   tile_format = "mlt",   min_zoom = 0L,   max_zoom = 14L,   base_zoom = NULL,   drop_rate = NULL,   cluster_distance = NULL,   cluster_maxzoom = NULL,   coalesce = FALSE,   simplification = TRUE,   overwrite = TRUE,   quiet = FALSE,   source_crs = NULL,   streaming = "auto" )

title: Create vector tiles from a DuckDB SQL query — freestile_query
text: query Character. SQL query returns geometry column. DuckDB spatial functions like ST_Read() read_parquet() available. output Character. Path output .pmtiles file. db_path Character. Path DuckDB database file, NULL (default) -memory database. layer_name Character. Name tile layer. NULL, derived output filename. tile_format Character. "mlt" (default) "mvt". min_zoom Integer. Minimum zoom level (default 0). max_zoom Integer. Maximum zoom level (default 14). base_zoom Integer. Zoom level features present. NULL (default) uses max_zoom. drop_rate Numeric. Exponential drop rate. NULL (default) disables. cluster_distance Numeric. Pixel distance clustering. NULL disables. cluster_maxzoom Integer. Max zoom clustering. Default max_zoom - 1. coalesce Logical. Whether merge features identical attributes (default FALSE). simplification Logical. Whether snap geometries tile pixel grid (default TRUE). overwrite Logical. Whether overwrite existing output (default TRUE). quiet Logical. Whether suppress progress (default FALSE). source_crs Character NULL. CRS geometry returned query, example "EPSG:4326" "EPSG:4267". Used R duckdb fallback; ignored Rust DuckDB backend. streaming Character. DuckDB query execution mode: "auto" (default) enables streaming point pipeline large queries, "always" forces , "never" uses existing -memory path.

title: Create vector tiles from a DuckDB SQL query — freestile_query
text: output file path (invisibly).

title: Create vector tiles from a DuckDB SQL query — freestile_query
text: using R fallback, source_crs must supplied explicitly query result can interpreted reprojected correctly. Pass "EPSG:4326" SQL already returns WGS84 geometry, source CRS string (example "EPSG:4267") DuckDB reproject WGS84 tiling. file-based input CRS embedded file, use freestile_file() engine = "duckdb" instead, auto-detects source CRS.

title: Create vector tiles from a DuckDB SQL query — freestile_query
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Query a GeoParquet file freestile_query(   "SELECT * FROM read_parquet('data.parquet') WHERE pop > 50000",   "output.pmtiles" )  # Query a Shapefile freestile_query(   "SELECT * FROM ST_Read('counties.shp')",   "counties.pmtiles" )  # Query with an existing DuckDB database freestile_query(   "SELECT * FROM my_table WHERE region = 'West'",   "west.pmtiles",   db_path = "my_database.duckdb" ) } # }

what: freestiler: Create Vector Tiles from Spatial Data — freestiler-package
title: freestiler: Create Vector Tiles from Spatial Data — freestiler-package
text: Create vector tile archives PMTiles format sf spatial data frames. Supports Mapbox Vector Tile (MVT) MapLibre Tile (MLT) output formats. Uses Rust backend via 'extendr' fast, -memory tiling zero external system dependencies.

title: freestiler: Create Vector Tiles from Spatial Data — freestiler-package
text: Maintainer: Kyle Walker kyle@walker-data.com

what: Serve PMTiles files via local HTTP server with CORS — serve_tiles
title: Serve PMTiles files via local HTTP server with CORS — serve_tiles
text: Start local HTTP server serve PMTiles files CORS headers HTTP range request support. allows PMTiles consumed mapgl MapLibre GL JS. server runs background can stopped stop_server().

title: Serve PMTiles files via local HTTP server with CORS — serve_tiles
code: serve_tiles(path, port = 8080)

title: Serve PMTiles files via local HTTP server with CORS — serve_tiles
text: path Path directory containing PMTiles files, single PMTiles file. single file, directory served. port Port number HTTP server. Default 8080.

title: Serve PMTiles files via local HTTP server with CORS — serve_tiles
text: Invisibly returns list url, port, dir. server handle stored internally can stopped stop_server().

title: Serve PMTiles files via local HTTP server with CORS — serve_tiles
text: server already running requested port, stopped first. server uses httpuv (dependency Shiny) serve static files CORS range-request headers PMTiles requires. Works well files ~1 GB. larger files, consider external server like npx http-server /path --cors -c-1.

title: Serve PMTiles files via local HTTP server with CORS — serve_tiles
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Serve a directory serve_tiles("/tmp/tiles")  # Serve a single file (its directory is served) serve_tiles("us_bgs.pmtiles")  # Stop when done stop_server() } # }

title: Stop a local tile server — stop_server

what: Quickly view a PMTiles file on an interactive map — view_tiles
title: Quickly view a PMTiles file on an interactive map — view_tiles
text: Starts local tile server (needed) creates interactive mapgl map showing tileset. Layer type styling auto-detected PMTiles metadata possible.

title: Quickly view a PMTiles file on an interactive map — view_tiles
code: view_tiles(   input,   layer = NULL,   layer_type = NULL,   color = NULL,   opacity = 0.5,   port = 8080,   promote_id = NULL )

title: Quickly view a PMTiles file on an interactive map — view_tiles
text: input Path local .pmtiles file. layer Character. Source layer name display. NULL (default), first layer tileset used. layer_type Character. Map layer type: "fill", "line", "circle". NULL, auto-detected PMTiles metadata geometry type. color Fill, line, circle color. Default "navy" fill line layers, "steelblue" circle layers. opacity Numeric opacity (0–1). Default 0.5. port Port local tile server. Default 8080. promote_id Character. Property name use feature ID hover interactivity. NULL, feature promotion used.

title: Quickly view a PMTiles file on an interactive map — view_tiles
text: mapgl map object (can piped mapgl operations).

title: Quickly view a PMTiles file on an interactive map — view_tiles
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ freestile(nc, "nc.pmtiles", layer_name = "counties") view_tiles("nc.pmtiles")  # Override auto-detection view_tiles("roads.pmtiles", layer_type = "line", color = "red")  # Point data view_tiles("airports.pmtiles", layer_type = "circle", color = "orange") } # }

title: freestiler 0.1.0
text: Initial release.

title: freestiler 0.1.0
text: freestile() creates PMTiles archives sf data frames zero external dependencies (tippecanoe, Java, Go). Supports MapLibre Tiles (MLT) Mapbox Vector Tiles (MVT) output formats. Multi-layer output via named lists freestile_layer() per-layer zoom control.

title: freestiler 0.1.0
text: POINT, MULTIPOINT, LINESTRING, MULTILINESTRING, POLYGON, MULTIPOLYGON. Automatic CRS transformation WGS84. Z/M dimension handling (dropped automatically).

title: freestiler 0.1.0
text: Parallel tile encoding rayon (across tiles within tiles). Tile-pixel grid snapping zoom-adaptive simplification without slivers. Buffered tile assignment clipping seamless tile boundaries.

title: freestiler 0.1.0
text: drop_rate exponential feature thinning Morton-curve spatial ordering points area-based ordering polygons/lines. base_zoom control ensuring features present higher zooms. cluster_distance point clustering point_count attribute. coalesce line merging polygon grouping.

title: freestiler 0.1.0
text: Spec-compliant MapLibre Tile encoder varint, delta, RLE, dictionary encoding. Validated mlt-core 0.1.2 reference decoder.
